mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, creating a positive feedback loop that lifts equity prices and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production expands for three consecutive months, it reflects robust manufacturing and utility output that translates into higher corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle or recessionary regimes, with direction accuracy dropping below 55% in those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained job creation and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation acceleration exceeding 0.4% in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where inflation crosses the 0.4% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation remains below 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and real GDP expansion, where industrial output outpaces GDP growth for two consecutive quarters, signals improving supply-side efficiency and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures decline while inflation remains elevated, households reduce discretionary spending to cover essential costs, eroding corporate revenue growth and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative 5-day returns in less than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rallies.
